label: Mathematical Logic
shortDescription: A free mathematical logic course on the foundations of mathematics: logic, proofs, set theory, models, computability, and incompleteness theorems.
modulesDescription: Read the mathematical logic chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogTitle: Learn Mathematical Logic for Free | CalcPath
ogDescription: Master the foundations: propositional and predicate logic, formal proofs, set theory, model theory, computability, Godel's theorems with 50+ practice questions and detailed explanations.
courseDescription: A rigorous introduction to mathematical logic covering propositional calculus, first-order logic, formal systems and proofs, ZFC set theory, model theory, computability via Turing machines, and Godel's incompleteness theorems. Includes ELI5 explanations, worked proof examples, common fallacies, and practice problems.

topics.title: Propositional Logic
topics.description: Propositional calculus, logical connectives, truth tables, tautologies, satisfiability, and normal forms.

topics.title: Predicate Logic
topics.description: First-order logic, predicates, quantifiers, free and bound variables, and interpretations.

topics.title: Formal Systems and Proofs
topics.description: Formal systems, inference rules, natural deduction, soundness, completeness, compactness, and decidability.

topics.title: Set Theory
topics.description: Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory, axiom of choice, ordinals, cardinals, transfinite induction, and well-ordering.

topics.title: Model Theory
topics.description: Structures, satisfaction, elementary equivalence, substructures, ultraproducts, and the completeness theorem.

topics.title: Computability Theory
topics.description: Turing machines, recursive functions, the halting problem, decidability, and Rice's theorem.

topics.title: Incompleteness and Independence
topics.description: Godel's incompleteness theorems, proof theory, forcing, and independence results in set theory.
